ddls:
CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_code" TEXT /* Code assigned to the region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the region */,
  "area_sq_km" REAL /* Area of the region in square kilometers */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "real_estate_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data entry */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region where the data was collected */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the data entry */,
  "average_price" REAL /* Average price of houses sold in the region */,
  "houses_sold" INTEGER /* Number of houses sold in the region */,
  "total_value" REAL /* Total value of houses sold in the region */,
  "property_type" TEXT /* Type of property (e.g., residential, commercial) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_real_estate_data_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "crime_data" (
  "crime_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each crime data entry */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region where the data was collected */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the crime data entry */,
  "no_of_crimes" INTEGER /* Number of crimes reported in the region */,
  "crime_rate" REAL /* Crime rate per 100,000 population */,
  "crime_type" TEXT /* Type of crime (e.g., violent, property) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("crime_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_crime_data_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, policymaker, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user (e.g., urban planning, public safety) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the real estate data entry being accessed */,
  "crime_id" INTEGER /* ID of the crime data entry being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "query_params" TEXT /* Query parameters used for filtering data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_crime_id FOREIGN KEY ("crime_id") REFERENCES crime_data ("crime_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES real_estate_data ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role that the permission is assigned to */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission (e.g., view_real_estate_data, edit_crime_data) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_permissions" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* ID of the permission assigned to the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "region_hierarchy" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* ID of the region */,
  "parent_region_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent region (null if it's a top-level region) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_region_hierarchy_parent_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)
